**NON-INTRUSIVE SIGNAL-BASED**

**ATTENDANCE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM**

_Software Requirements Specification (SRS) - Version 3.0_

Architecture: Campus LAN / UDP Broadcast

Attendance Statuses: PRESENT · LATE · ABSENT (auto-computed)

Final Year Project - Computer Science

Academic Year 2025 / 2026

# **1\. Introduction**

## **1.1 Purpose**

This document specifies the complete software requirements for the Non-Intrusive Signal-Based Attendance Management System (NISAMS). The system automates classroom attendance for university environments with class sizes of 100 or more students. It uses campus WiFi LAN discovery via UDP broadcast, time-windowed sessions, automatic absence computation, and offline-first data persistence. This version supersedes all earlier BLE and mobile-hotspot designs.

## **1.2 Scope**

NISAMS operates in three phases during any class period. In the Present window (first 0-10 minutes), the lecturer starts a session and the system broadcasts its availability to all students on the same network subnet. In the Late window (minutes 10-30), the session remains open at a reduced broadcast rate for students who arrive after the initial window. After minute 30, the session closes entirely and any enrolled student who has not submitted a record is automatically marked Absent by the system. All in-session traffic uses the local campus network only. Internet connectivity is required only for post-session cloud synchronisation, which is handled automatically.

## **1.3 Design Decisions and Rationale**

| **Design Question**                     | **Rejected Option**                                                 | **Adopted Solution**                                                             |
| --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| How to detect student presence?         | QR codes - interrupt lecture flow; BLE - spoofable, range ambiguous | UDP broadcast on campus LAN subnet - room boundary enforced at network layer     |
| How to support 100+ students?           | Mobile hotspot - Android cap of 8-10 devices                        | Campus WiFi - unlimited concurrent connections, no extra hardware                |
| How long should session broadcast run?  | Full lecture (3-4 hrs) - excessive battery drain                    | Windowed: 10 min Present + 20 min Late then full stop                            |
| How is absence detected?                | Manual roll-call or student self-report                             | Auto-computed: enrolled students minus submitted records after session closes    |
| What about late arrivals?               | Single fixed window - late students auto-absent                     | Two windows: Present (0-10 min) and Late (10-30 min) with reduced broadcast rate |
| What if a student arrives after 30 min? | No recourse - marked absent                                         | Lecturer manual override with audit trail - flagged MANUAL in reports            |
| How to prevent proxy attendance?        | Session ID alone - shareable                                        | Device fingerprint + biometric/PIN + session expiry window (non-transferable)    |

## **1.4 Definitions**

| **Term**               | **Definition**                                                                                                                                                           |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **UDP Broadcast**      | A UDP packet sent to 255.255.255.255 that reaches all devices on the same network subnet but is blocked at router boundaries, providing natural room isolation.          |
| **LAN**                | Local Area Network - the campus WiFi to which all students and lecturers connect.                                                                                        |
| **Subnet**             | A logical segment of the campus network. UDP broadcasts do not cross subnets, restricting session visibility to physical rooms served by the same router segment.        |
| **Present Window**     | The first 0-10 minutes of a session during which attendance is marked PRESENT. UDP broadcast interval: every 3 seconds.                                                  |
| **Late Window**        | Minutes 10-30 of a session during which attendance is marked LATE. UDP broadcast interval: every 20 seconds. Reduces battery usage by ~80% versus the Present window.    |
| **Auto-Absent**        | After the Late window closes, the system computes absence by querying all enrolled students for the course and flagging those with no ATTENDANCE_RECORD for the session. |
| **Session**            | A lecturer-initiated attendance window identified by a UUID session ID, course code, room code, and two expiry timestamps (Present and Late cutoffs).                    |
| **Offline-first**      | All data is persisted locally first. Cloud synchronisation occurs automatically when internet connectivity is available.                                                 |
| **Device Fingerprint** | A hash derived from device hardware identifiers that uniquely identifies a registered student device.                                                                    |
| **Manual Override**    | A lecturer action that marks a student PRESENT, LATE, or EXCUSED after the automated windows have closed. Recorded separately in the audit log.                          |
| **Room Code**          | A short alphanumeric code announced verbally by the lecturer. Required on flat-subnet networks where UDP reaches the entire campus.                                      |
| **Proxy Attendance**   | Attendance marked by a person not physically present. Mitigated by device binding, biometric confirmation, and session expiry.                                           |

# **2\. Overall Description**

## **2.1 Product Perspective**

NISAMS is a mobile client-server attendance platform with three tiers: (1) the student Android app, (2) the lecturer Android app that doubles as a local LAN server during sessions, and (3) a cloud backend for permanent storage and reporting. The local server tier is the key innovation - it removes the dependency on internet connectivity during live sessions and enables the system to handle 100 or more students simultaneously using existing campus infrastructure.

## **2.2 Core Product Functions**

- Lecturer starts a session for a selected course; app generates a session ID and starts a local HTTP server on the campus WiFi.
- Session ID is broadcast via UDP every 3 seconds during the Present window and every 20 seconds during the Late window.
- Student app detects the broadcast automatically and displays the session card with no manual input required.
- Student confirms attendance using PIN or device biometric. App submits a signed record to the lecturer's local server over the LAN.
- After the Late window expires, the lecturer's app computes the absent list by comparing enrolled students against submitted records.
- Lecturer can manually mark any enrolled student at any point during the session with full audit trail.
- After the session ends, the student app stops all network activity until the next session (post-confirmation sleep).
- Lecturer's app syncs all records to the cloud backend automatically when internet is available.
- Lecturers and department heads generate and export attendance reports in PDF and CSV formats.
- Admin manages users, devices, courses, and timetables.

## **2.3 User Classes**

| **User**            | **Role & Access**                                                                                                                    |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Student**         | Connects to campus WiFi, opens app, confirms attendance, views personal attendance history and percentage per course.                |
| **Lecturer**        | Creates and ends sessions, monitors live attendance count with PRESENT/LATE breakdown, triggers manual overrides, generates reports. |
| **Department Head** | Views aggregate attendance reports across all courses and lecturers in the department. Read-only access.                             |
| **Admin**           | Manages user accounts, registers and deregisters student devices, manages courses and timetables, views system-wide audit logs.      |

## **2.4 Operating Environment**

Primary client devices: Android 8.0 and above, including budget devices common in East African universities. Both lecturer and student devices must be connected to campus WiFi during sessions. The local HTTP server runs on the lecturer's phone during the session window only (maximum 30 minutes active). Cloud backend: Firebase Firestore, Supabase PostgreSQL, or a Node.js/Express REST server hosted on any cloud provider.

## **2.5 Constraints**

- Must support Android 8.0 and above.
- Must scale to 100 or more simultaneous student submissions without degradation.
- Must require no dedicated hardware beyond existing campus WiFi infrastructure.
- Must minimise mobile data - all in-session traffic uses the campus LAN only.
- Must preserve all attendance records if internet is unavailable during and after the session.
- If the campus uses a flat subnet (all devices on one segment), a verbally announced room code supplements network-level room isolation.
- Lecturer's app active battery window must not exceed 30 minutes per class to remain practical for a 3-4 hour lecture day.

## **2.6 Assumptions**

- Campus WiFi is present in all lecture rooms, even if outbound internet access via that WiFi is intermittent.
- All students and lecturers carry Android smartphones running Android 8.0 or above with WiFi support.
- Students have registered their devices with the system at enrolment.
- Users will grant the required permissions: local network access, optional biometric sensor.
- Attendance for a given class is taken once at the start, not repeatedly throughout the lecture.

# **3\. Specific Requirements**

## **3.1 Functional Requirements**

| **ID** | **Title** | **Priority** | **Description** |
| ------ | --------- | ------------ | --------------- |

### **Session & Broadcast**

| **FR01** | **Session Creation** | **High** | The system shall allow a lecturer to create an attendance session by selecting a course. The app generates a UUID session ID, a Present cutoff timestamp (configurable, default 10 minutes), and a Late cutoff timestamp (configurable, default 30 minutes from session start). A lightweight HTTP server starts on the lecturer's device within the campus WiFi network. |
| -------- | -------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR02** | **UDP Present Broadcast** | **High** | During the Present window (0 to 10 minutes), the lecturer's device shall broadcast a UDP packet to 255.255.255.255 on port 5501 every 3 seconds. The packet shall contain: session ID, course code, lecturer ID, room code, Present cutoff time, and Late cutoff time. |
| -------- | ------------------------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR03** | **UDP Late Broadcast** | **High** | During the Late window (10 to 30 minutes), the lecturer's device shall continue broadcasting the same packet but at a reduced interval of every 20 seconds. This reduces radio activity by approximately 85 percent compared to the Present window. |
| -------- | ---------------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR04** | **Session Auto-Close** | **High** | At the Late cutoff timestamp, the system shall automatically stop UDP broadcasting and shut down the local HTTP server without requiring any action from the lecturer. The session status shall be updated to CLOSED in local storage. |
| -------- | ---------------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

### **Student Discovery & Confirmation**

| **FR05** | **Session Discovery** | **High** | The student app shall listen on UDP port 5501 while open. Upon receiving a valid broadcast, it shall display a session card showing course name, lecturer name, current window status (PRESENT or LATE), and remaining time. No manual input is required from the student. |
| -------- | --------------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR06** | **Room Code Verification** | **High** | The student app shall compare the room code in the received UDP packet against a room code entered by the student at app launch (or provided via the lecturer's verbal announcement). Submissions with mismatched room codes shall be rejected by the lecturer's server. |
| -------- | -------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |

| **FR07** | **Attendance Confirmation** | **High** | The system shall require the student to confirm attendance using PIN or device biometric before submission. This check occurs locally on the student device. No credentials are transmitted over the network. |
| -------- | --------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR08** | **Attendance Submission** | **High** | After confirmation, the student app shall send an HTTP POST to the lecturer's local IP address containing: student ID, device fingerprint hash, session ID, room code, window status at time of submission (PRESENT or LATE), and a UTC timestamp. The submission port shall match the lecturer's server port. |
| -------- | ------------------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR09** | **Post-Confirmation Sleep** | **High** | Once the student's attendance is confirmed and acknowledged by the lecturer's server, the student app shall stop UDP listening and release all network resources. The app shall not perform any background network activity for the remainder of the lecture. This eliminates battery drain for the 3-4 hour lecture period. |
| -------- | --------------------------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

### **Server-Side Validation & Storage**

| **FR10** | **Record Validation** | **High** | The lecturer's local HTTP server shall validate each incoming record against four checks: (1) session ID matches the active session, (2) session has not expired, (3) device fingerprint is registered to the claiming student, and (4) room code matches. Records failing any check are rejected with an appropriate error code returned to the student app. |
| -------- | --------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR11** | **Local Storage** | **High** | Valid attendance records shall be stored in a SQLite database on the lecturer's device immediately upon receipt. Records shall include: record ID, session ID, student ID, device fingerprint, submission timestamp, and window status (PRESENT or LATE). |
| -------- | ----------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR12** | **Live Attendance Count** | **High** | The lecturer's interface shall display in real time the count of students marked PRESENT and the count marked LATE, updating each time a new record is stored. The total enrolled count shall also be displayed so the lecturer can see how many students are yet to submit. |
| -------- | ------------------------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

### **Absence Computation**

| **FR13** | **Auto-Absent Computation** | **High** | When the session transitions to CLOSED (at the Late cutoff), the system shall compute the absent list by performing a LEFT JOIN between the ENROLMENT table for the current course and the ATTENDANCE_RECORD table for the current session. Any enrolled student with no matching attendance record shall have an ABSENT record created automatically with the session close timestamp. |
| -------- | --------------------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR14** | **Absent List Display** | **High** | The lecturer's interface shall display the full absent list after session close, showing each absent student's name and registration number. The list shall be exportable as part of the session report. |
| -------- | ----------------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

### **Manual Override**

| **FR15** | **Lecturer Manual Override** | **High** | The lecturer shall be able to search for any enrolled student by name or registration number from the session dashboard and manually set their status to PRESENT, LATE, or EXCUSED at any point during or after the session window. Manual records shall be stored with a MANUAL flag and logged in the audit trail with the lecturer ID, student ID, new status, and timestamp. |
| -------- | ---------------------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

### **Synchronisation & Reporting**

| **FR16** | **Cloud Synchronisation** | **High** | When internet connectivity is detected, the lecturer's app shall automatically batch-sync all unsynced attendance records to the cloud backend via HTTPS POST. The server shall deduplicate records using a composite key of session ID and student ID. Successfully synced records shall be marked as synced in local storage. |
| -------- | ------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR17** | **Conflict Resolution** | **Medium** | If two records for the same student and session arrive during sync (possible if a student submitted twice before expiry was enforced), the server shall retain the record with the earlier submission timestamp and discard the later one. |
| -------- | ----------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |

| **FR18** | **Attendance Reports** | **High** | The system shall generate attendance reports filterable by course, date range, and student. Reports shall display PRESENT, LATE, ABSENT, EXCUSED, and MANUAL counts per session and cumulative percentages per student. Export formats: PDF and CSV. |
| -------- | ---------------------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR19** | **Student Attendance View** | **Medium** | Students shall be able to view their personal attendance history per enrolled course, including: sessions attended, sessions late, sessions absent, and overall attendance percentage. |
| -------- | --------------------------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR20** | **User & Device Management** | **High** | The admin shall be able to create, update, and deactivate student and lecturer accounts; register and deregister student devices; manage course enrolments; and configure system-wide session window defaults. |
| -------- | ---------------------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| **FR21** | **Audit Logging** | **Medium** | The system shall log: session creation, UDP broadcast start/stop, each attendance submission (with status), manual overrides, sync events, and report generation. Logs are immutable and accessible to admins and department heads. |
| -------- | ----------------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

## **3.2 Non-Functional Requirements**

| **ID**    | **Category**       | **Requirement**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| --------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **NFR01** | Performance        | Session detection shall appear on the student's screen within 5 seconds of the student opening the app in the classroom. Attendance submission shall complete within 3 seconds of biometric/PIN confirmation.                                                                        |
| **NFR02** | Scalability        | The lecturer's local HTTP server shall handle 100 or more concurrent HTTP POST submissions without queue overflow or record loss. The server shall process submissions sequentially with a thread-safe SQLite write queue.                                                           |
| **NFR03** | Energy - Lecturer  | The local HTTP server and UDP broadcast shall be active for a maximum of 30 minutes per session. Outside this window, no network services shall run on the lecturer's device related to NISAMS.                                                                                      |
| **NFR04** | Energy - Student   | The student app shall stop all background network activity immediately after attendance is confirmed. UDP listening shall not run when the app is in the background after confirmation.                                                                                              |
| **NFR05** | Data Efficiency    | All in-session traffic shall use the campus LAN only, consuming zero mobile data. Cloud sync payloads shall be under 1 KB per attendance record. Total sync data per 100-student session: under 100 KB.                                                                              |
| **NFR06** | Security           | Proxy attendance shall be mitigated through: device fingerprint binding (registered at enrolment), session expiry windows (30-minute maximum), room code verification, and local biometric/PIN confirmation. No credentials or biometric data shall be transmitted over the network. |
| **NFR07** | Reliability        | No attendance record shall be lost during network interruption, app crash, or device power loss. SQLite write operations shall use transactions with rollback support.                                                                                                               |
| **NFR08** | Offline Capability | The system shall operate fully without internet during a session. The lecturer's app shall display live attendance counts from local storage. Cloud sync shall be deferred and automatic.                                                                                            |
| **NFR09** | Compatibility      | The app shall support Android 8.0 and above, targeting devices with 2 GB RAM or more.                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| **NFR10** | Usability          | The student attendance flow shall require no more than three taps from app open to confirmed submission. The lecturer session flow shall require no more than two taps from app open to session active.                                                                              |
| **NFR11** | Maintainability    | The codebase shall be modular with separate layers for: UDP service, HTTP server, local persistence, cloud sync, and UI. Each module shall be independently testable.                                                                                                                |

# **4\. External Interface Requirements**

## **4.1 User Interfaces**

### **4.1.1 Lecturer App Screens**

- Home: course list with Start Session button per course.
- Live Session: real-time PRESENT count, LATE count, enrolled total, remaining window time, and manual override search bar.
- Session Closed: full result breakdown - PRESENT, LATE, ABSENT, MANUAL - with per-student absent list and export controls.
- History: past session list with per-session drill-down and PDF/CSV export.
- Sync Status: indicator showing pending unsynced records and last sync timestamp.

### **4.1.2 Student App Screens**

- Home: room code entry field (if required) and session detection status.
- Session Detected: session card with course name, lecturer, window status (PRESENT/LATE), and countdown timer.
- Confirmation: PIN/biometric prompt.
- Confirmed: attendance acknowledged screen with timestamp and status badge.
- History: personal attendance list per course with percentage.

### **4.1.3 Admin Web Panel**

- User management: create, edit, deactivate students and lecturers.
- Device registration: link and unlink devices to student accounts.
- Course and enrolment management.
- Audit log viewer with filtering.

## **4.2 Hardware Interfaces**

The system uses only the smartphone WiFi radio and, where available, the device fingerprint or face recognition sensor. No dedicated hardware is required beyond the existing campus WiFi access points.

## **4.3 Software Interfaces**

Mobile app communicates with a cloud backend via HTTPS REST API. Local persistence uses SQLite via Android Room. UDP socket operations use Android's DatagramSocket API. The local HTTP server is a lightweight embedded server (e.g. NanoHTTPD or Ktor) running within the lecturer app process.

## **4.4 Communication Protocols**

| **Channel**           | **Protocol**    | **Notes**                                                           |
| --------------------- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Session discovery     | UDP broadcast   | Port 5501, subnet-limited, no internet required                     |
| Attendance submission | HTTP POST (LAN) | Student to lecturer's local server, port 5500, no internet required |
| Cloud synchronisation | HTTPS REST      | Lecturer's app to cloud backend, triggered when internet available  |
| Admin / reporting     | HTTPS REST      | Web browser or app to cloud backend                                 |

# **5\. System Architecture & Session Lifecycle**

## **5.1 Full Session Flow**

- Lecturer opens app and selects course. App joins campus WiFi (already connected) and starts local HTTP server on port 5500. Present window begins.
- App broadcasts UDP packet to 255.255.255.255:5501 every 3 seconds. Packet contains: session ID, course code, room code, Present cutoff, Late cutoff.
- All student apps on the same subnet receive the broadcast and display the session card automatically. Students enter room code if prompted, then authenticate via PIN or biometric.
- Student app sends HTTP POST to lecturer's local IP. Server validates: session ID, expiry, device fingerprint, room code. On success, record is stored in SQLite with status PRESENT. Live count updates on lecturer screen.
- At minute 10, the system transitions to the Late window. Broadcast interval changes to every 20 seconds. New submissions from this point are stored with status LATE.
- At minute 30, the session auto-closes. UDP broadcasting stops. HTTP server stops. The system runs the absent query: all enrolled students for this course minus all attendance records for this session = ABSENT list.
- Lecturer reviews the session result (PRESENT count, LATE count, ABSENT list). Manually marks any student as needed. Session is complete.
- When internet connectivity is detected, the app automatically syncs all records to the cloud backend. Sync is batched and deduplicated.

## **5.2 Energy Budget Per Session**

| **Activity**                       | **Duration**        | **Est. Battery**         | **After Window**                  |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------- | ------------------------ | --------------------------------- |
| UDP broadcast (Present) - lecturer | 0-10 min            | **~1%**                  | Stops completely                  |
| UDP broadcast (Late) - lecturer    | 10-30 min           | **~0.5%**                | Stops completely                  |
| HTTP server - lecturer             | 0-30 min            | **~1%**                  | Stops completely                  |
| UDP listening - student            | Until confirmed     | **~0.5%**                | Stops immediately on confirmation |
| Cloud sync - lecturer              | Burst after session | **~0.5%**                | One-time, background              |
| Lecture continues (all roles)      | 30 min - 4 hrs      | **0% (no app activity)** | Phones in pockets                 |

Total estimated battery impact per session: 3-4% on lecturer device, 1-2% on student device. Negligible over a full lecture day.

## **5.3 Room Isolation Layers**

| **Layer**                   | **How it prevents cross-room attendance**                                                                                                  |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Network subnet**          | UDP broadcasts are stopped at router boundaries. Students in the next room on a different subnet never receive the packet.                 |
| **Room code**               | A short code announced verbally by the lecturer. Effective fallback on flat-subnet campuses where UDP reaches all devices.                 |
| **Session expiry (30 min)** | Even if a student outside the room captures the UDP packet, the session ID expires. Replay attacks are ineffective.                        |
| **Device fingerprint**      | Each submission is tied to a registered device. Sharing session details with an absent friend is useless - their device is not registered. |
| **Biometric / PIN**         | The physical device owner must confirm on their own device. The device cannot be handed to someone else without unlocking it.              |

# **6\. Data Flow Diagrams (Mermaid)**

## **6.1 Level 0 - Context Diagram**

**6.3 Level 2 - Sub-process Detail**

![mermaid-ai-diagram-2026-05-13-101239](data:image/png;base64,)

![mermaid-ai-diagram-2026-05-13-101435](data:image/png;base64,)

![mermaid-ai-diagram-2026-05-13-101255](data:image/png;base64,)

![mermaid-ai-diagram-2026-05-13-101304](data:image/png;base64,)

![mermaid-ai-diagram-2026-05-13-101448](data:image/png;base64,)

![mermaid-ai-diagram-2026-05-13-101316](data:image/png;base64,)

# **7\. Entity Relationship Diagram (Mermaid)**

![Attendance Logging Flow-2026-05-13-103847](data:image/png;base64,)

## **7.1 Entity Notes**

| **Entity**            | **Key Design Notes**                                                                                                                                                                      |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **SESSION**           | status enum: ACTIVE_PRESENT \| ACTIVE_LATE \| CLOSED. present_cutoff and late_cutoff are stored so the server can validate submissions even if the broadcaster has stopped.               |
| **ATTENDANCE_RECORD** | status enum: PRESENT (submitted in 0-10 min), LATE (10-30 min), ABSENT (auto-computed on close), MANUAL (lecturer override), EXCUSED (admin-granted). is_synced tracks cloud push status. |
| **DEVICE**            | is_active flag allows device deregistration without deleting history. Fingerprint hash is computed from hardware identifiers at registration - never biometric data.                      |
| **ENROLMENT**         | Source of truth for absent computation. The LEFT JOIN in FR13 uses this table to find students with no attendance record.                                                                 |
| **AUDIT_LOG**         | Immutable. details field stores JSON context (e.g. previous status for manual overrides). Used for academic integrity investigations.                                                     |

# **8\. Use Case Diagram (Mermaid)**

![Attendance Logging Flow-2026-05-13-104358](data:image/png;base64,)

## **8.1 Use Case Narratives**

| **UC02: Start Attendance Session** | **Primary Actor: Lecturer**                                                                                                                                    |
| ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Precondition**                   | Lecturer app open, connected to campus WiFi, course selected.                                                                                                  |
| **Main Flow**                      | Lecturer taps Start Session. App generates session ID, present_cutoff, late_cutoff. Local HTTP server starts on port 5500. UDP broadcast begins (3s interval). |
| **Postcondition**                  | Session is ACTIVE_PRESENT. UDP packet circulating on subnet.                                                                                                   |

| **UC04: Detect Active Session** | **Primary Actor: Student**                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Precondition**                | Student app open, connected to same campus WiFi subnet.                                                                                                          |
| **Main Flow**                   | App listens on UDP port 5501. Receives broadcast packet. Validates session is not expired. Displays session card with course name, window status, and countdown. |
| **Postcondition**               | Session card visible. Student can proceed to confirmation.                                                                                                       |

| **UC08: Enter Late Window** | **Primary Actor: System (auto)**                                                                                                                             |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Precondition**            | Session has been active for 10 minutes (present_cutoff reached).                                                                                             |
| **Main Flow**               | System transitions session status to ACTIVE_LATE. UDP broadcast interval changes from 3 seconds to 20 seconds. Lecturer screen updates window label to LATE. |
| **Postcondition**           | Session still accepting submissions. New submissions will be marked LATE.                                                                                    |

| **UC10: Compute Absent List** | **Primary Actor: System (auto)**                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Precondition**              | Session has reached late_cutoff (30 minutes elapsed).                                                                                                                                                                                |
| **Main Flow**                 | System queries ENROLMENT for all students enrolled in the course. Performs LEFT JOIN with ATTENDANCE_RECORD for the session ID. For each enrolled student with no record, creates an ABSENT record with the session close timestamp. |
| **Postcondition**             | All enrolled students have an attendance record (PRESENT, LATE, or ABSENT). Lecturer sees full result summary.                                                                                                                       |

| **UC12: Manual Override** | **Primary Actor: Lecturer**                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Precondition**          | Session is in any state (ACTIVE or CLOSED). Student is enrolled in the course.                                                                                                                                                                   |
| **Main Flow**             | Lecturer searches student by name or ID. Selects new status (PRESENT, LATE, EXCUSED). System creates or updates ATTENDANCE_RECORD with MANUAL flag. Audit log entry created with lecturer ID, student ID, old status, new status, and timestamp. |
| **Postcondition**         | Student attendance record updated. Change visible in reports with MANUAL label.                                                                                                                                                                  |

| **UC13: Sync Records to Cloud** | **Primary Actor: System (auto)**                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Precondition**                | Internet connectivity detected on lecturer's device.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| **Main Flow**                   | App reads all records where is_synced = false from SQLite. Batches into groups of 50. Sends HTTPS POST to cloud API. Server deduplicates by session_id + student_id. Successful records marked is_synced = true in local DB. |
| **Postcondition**               | Cloud backend holds complete attendance data. Local SQLite retains records as backup.                                                                                                                                        |

# **9\. Defense Preparation**

## **9.1 Anticipated Examiner Questions**

| **Examiner Question**                                          | **Model Answer**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| -------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| How does the system handle late students?                      | The session has two windows: Present (0-10 min) for full marks and Late (10-30 min) for flagged attendance. After 30 minutes the session closes automatically. Lecturers can manually override any student at any point with a full audit trail.                                                                   |
| How does the system know who is absent?                        | Absence is never self-reported. After the session closes, the system runs a LEFT JOIN between the ENROLMENT table (everyone who should attend) and ATTENDANCE_RECORD (everyone who submitted). Anyone with no matching record is automatically marked ABSENT. No student action needed.                            |
| Does the app drain the lecturer's battery in a 4-hour lecture? | No. The system is active for a maximum of 30 minutes per session - the Present and Late windows combined. After that, all broadcasting and the HTTP server stop completely. The remaining 3+ hours of lecture require zero app activity. Total battery cost is under 4%.                                           |
| What stops a student in the next room from marking attendance? | Four layers: (1) UDP broadcasts stop at subnet router boundaries, so students on a different network segment never receive the packet; (2) a room code announced verbally by the lecturer filters flat-subnet campuses; (3) session IDs expire within 30 minutes; (4) submissions are bound to registered devices. |
| What if the campus is on one flat subnet?                      | The room code mechanism handles this. The lecturer announces a short alphanumeric code verbally at the start of class. The server rejects submissions with incorrect room codes. This is documented as a known constraint in the SRS.                                                                              |
| What about a student who lends their phone to a friend?        | The device fingerprint binds the submission to a registered device, but the student must also pass biometric or PIN confirmation on that device. Lending the phone means handing over the device and the PIN - a deliberate act the lecturer's manual override and audit log can flag if suspected.                |
| Why not use BLE instead of WiFi?                               | BLE signals can be spoofed by replaying advertisement packets from outside the room. Range is also ambiguous - 10 metres is approximate and depends on walls. UDP on the campus LAN enforces room boundaries at the network layer, which is architecturally stronger.                                              |
| How does the system handle 100+ students simultaneously?       | The lecturer's embedded HTTP server uses a thread-safe queue to process submissions sequentially into SQLite. Network throughput over WiFi easily handles 100 small HTTP POST requests in parallel. Load testing with simulated submissions is part of our planned evaluation.                                     |
| What happens if the internet is down all day?                  | All records are stored in SQLite on the lecturer's phone. The lecturer can see the full results locally including the absent list. Cloud sync runs automatically the next time internet is available. No data is lost.                                                                                             |

## **9.2 Recommended Minimum Demo**

- Lecturer phone starts a session - show session ID generated and UDP broadcasting on screen.
- Student phone (same WiFi) auto-detects session - session card appears without manual input.
- Student enters room code and authenticates with PIN - attendance confirmed, live count updates on lecturer screen.
- Wait for Present window to expire - screen changes to LATE banner.
- Close session - absent list is computed and displayed automatically.
- Simulate sync - disable WiFi, show pending count; re-enable WiFi, show sync completing.

## **9.3 Scope Boundaries (What the System Does Not Do)**

- The system does not manage the lecture timetable - it relies on manual course selection by the lecturer. Timetable integration is listed as a future enhancement.
- The system does not enforce that students remain in the room for the full lecture - it records presence at the time of submission only.
- The system does not transmit or store biometric data - biometric confirmation is handled entirely by the device OS and only returns a pass/fail result to the app.
